metadata.context: EKS SaaS GitOps monorepo with Terraform IaC, Karpenter, and multi-tenant infrastructure. Classified as infrastructure-only to test N/A mappings, everything that is not serverless will run here, EKS will be the centralized platform

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing instrumented in infrastructure or GitOps configuration
top_gaps.impact: Cannot trace requests across multi-tenant services; debugging requires manual log correlation

top_gaps.title: Business Metrics
top_gaps.gap: No custom business metrics — only infrastructure-level monitoring via Kubecost/Prometheus
top_gaps.impact: Cannot measure tenant SaaS outcomes to inform modernization decisions

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail configuration despite provisioning account-level resources
top_gaps.impact: Cannot audit API calls to AWS resources provisioned by this infrastructure

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: SSM SecureString used but no automated rotation configured
top_gaps.impact: Credentials may become stale; no rotation policy for long-lived secrets

top_gaps.title: Incident Response Automation
top_gaps.gap: No runbooks or automated incident response workflows defined
top_gaps.impact: Incident response is entirely ad hoc with no self-healing patterns

findings.title: Workflow Orchestration — Partial Adoption
findings.description: Argo Workflows and Argo Events deployed for tenant lifecycle automation but no application-level business workflow orchestration (Step Functions) present.
findings.gap: Workflow orchestration covers infrastructure automation but not application business logic workflows.
findings.recommendation: Consider AWS Step Functions or extending Argo Workflows for business-critical multi-step operations as application services mature on this platform.

findings.title: Network Security — Public EKS Endpoint
findings.description: VPC with 3 AZs and private subnets for EKS workers. However, EKS cluster endpoint is publicly accessible and no VPC endpoints configured.
findings.gap: EKS cluster API endpoint publicly accessible. No VPC endpoints for AWS services.
findings.recommendation: Restrict EKS cluster endpoint to private access. Add VPC endpoints for ECR, STS, S3, DynamoDB, SQS.

findings.title: API Entry Point — ALB Without Advanced Controls
findings.description: AWS Load Balancer Controller deployed for ALB ingress but no API Gateway, throttling, or request validation at entry point.
findings.gap: No throttling, authentication, or request validation at the entry point level.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS WAF on the ALB. Evaluate API Gateway for per-tenant throttling and API key management.

findings.title: Auto-Scaling — HPA Disabled by Default
findings.description: Karpenter provides mature node-level scaling but HPA is disabled by default in application Helm charts. No custom scaling metrics.
findings.gap: HPA not enabled by default. No custom metrics for scaling decisions.
findings.recommendation: Enable HPA by default in tenant Helm charts. Consider custom metrics (queue depth, requests-per-second) for scaling.

findings.title: Backup and Recovery — No Restore Procedures
findings.description: DynamoDB PITR enabled but no documented restore procedures, no cross-region backup, no S3 versioning, and no Gitea backup strategy.
findings.gap: No documented restore procedures. Gitea (source of truth) has no backup strategy.
findings.recommendation: Add S3 versioning. Implement Gitea backup. Document DynamoDB PITR restore procedures. Consider AWS Backup.

findings.title: High Availability — Single NAT Gateway
findings.description: EKS spans 3 AZs with DynamoDB multi-AZ and NATS 3 replicas. However, single NAT Gateway is a SPOF. Gitea is single-instance.
findings.gap: Single NAT Gateway creates AZ-failure risk for outbound connectivity. Gitea has no HA.
findings.recommendation: Deploy NAT Gateways per AZ. Evaluate managed Git service or HA Gitea deployment.

findings.title: IaC Coverage — Operational Resources Missing
findings.description: Comprehensive IaC for compute, networking, databases, and GitOps. However, no IaC for monitoring, alarms, backup plans, or WAF.
findings.gap: Operational/DR resources (alarms, backup plans, health checks, WAF) not in IaC. Estimated 70-85% coverage.
findings.recommendation: Add Terraform resources for CloudWatch alarms, AWS Backup plans, Route 53 health checks, and WAF rules.

findings.title: CI/CD Automation — No Quality Gates
findings.description: Multi-layered CI/CD with Gitea Actions, Flux CD, Image Automation, and Argo Workflows. However, no automated testing, no terraform validate/plan in CI, no security scanning.
findings.gap: No automated testing in CI. No terraform plan/validate in CI. No security scanning in build pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add terraform validate/plan, container image scanning, and linting as CI gates.

findings.title: Audit Logging — No CloudTrail
findings.description: No CloudTrail configuration in repository despite provisioning account-level resources (EKS, VPC, IAM). EKS control plane logging not enabled.
findings.gap: No CloudTrail. No EKS control plane audit logging. No immutable log storage.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Enable EKS control plane logging.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest — AWS-Managed Keys Only
findings.description: All data stores use AWS-managed encryption (SQS SSE, DynamoDB default, EBS CSI). No customer-managed KMS keys configured.
findings.gap: No customer-managed KMS keys. Less control over rotation and access auditing.
findings.recommendation: Create customer-managed KMS keys for sensitive data. Define key rotation policies.

findings.title: API Authentication — No External Auth at Ingress
findings.description: IRSA provides pod-level AWS authentication. However, no OAuth2/JWT/Cognito authentication at ALB ingress for tenant-facing endpoints.
findings.gap: No application-level API authentication at the ingress layer.
findings.recommendation: Add Cognito or OIDC authentication at ALB level for tenant-facing APIs.

findings.title: Centralized Identity — No IdP Integration
findings.description: No centralized identity provider integration. Gitea uses local auth. EKS uses IAM. No SSO or federated identity.
findings.gap: Each component manages identity independently. No centralized IdP.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Amazon Cognito for tenant apps. Federate EKS access with organizational IdP via OIDC.

findings.title: Secrets Management — No Rotation
findings.description: SSM SecureString for Gitea password. Kubernetes Secrets for Flux credentials. No plaintext in source. But no Secrets Manager, no rotation.
findings.gap: No automated rotation. SSM used but Secrets Manager with rotation would be more appropriate.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Secrets Manager with rotation. Add External Secrets Operator for K8s sync.

findings.title: Compute Hardening — No Vulnerability Scanning
findings.description: Karpenter uses al2023@latest AMI. SSM policy attached. But no Patch Manager, no vulnerability scanning, no hardened AMI pipeline.
findings.gap: No automated patching beyond latest AMI. No container or node vulnerability scanning.
findings.recommendation: Enable ECR scanning. Add Trivy to CI. Configure SSM Patch Manager. Consider Bottlerocket.

findings.title: Security Pipeline — Checkov Referenced But Not in CI
findings.description: Checkov skip annotations in Terraform indicate awareness but no security scanning integrated into Gitea Actions CI pipeline.
findings.gap: Checkov not in CI. No container scanning. No dependency scanning. No security gates.
findings.recommendation: Add Checkov/tfsec to Terraform CI. Add Trivy for containers. Add pip-audit for Python deps.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing — Not Instrumented
findings.description: No distributed tracing (X-Ray, OpenTelemetry, Jaeger) found in any IaC, Helm chart, or GitOps manifest.
findings.gap: Complete absence of distributed tracing in a multi-tenant, multi-service architecture.
findings.recommendation: Deploy ADOT as DaemonSet. Instrument microservices with OpenTelemetry SDK. Enable X-Ray for SQS/DynamoDB.

findings.title: SLO Definitions — Implicit Only
findings.description: Prometheus deployed via Kubecost but no formal SLO definitions, no latency/error-rate alarms, no error budgets.
findings.gap: No formal SLOs despite having monitoring infrastructure (Prometheus).
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for tenant onboarding latency, message processing, and API availability per tier.

findings.title: Business Metrics — Not Published
findings.description: No custom business metrics. Kubecost tracks costs but no tenant onboarding rates, message throughput, or per-tier utilization.
findings.gap: Cannot measure SaaS platform health from a business perspective.
findings.recommendation: Add custom Prometheus metrics in microservices. Create Grafana dashboards for business KPIs.

findings.title: Anomaly Detection — Infrastructure Exists But Unused
findings.description: Prometheus deployed but no alerting rules (PrometheusRule CRDs), no anomaly detection, no PagerDuty integration.
findings.gap: Alerting infrastructure exists but no alert rules defined.
findings.recommendation: Define PrometheusRule CRDs. Add Alertmanager with notification integration. Add CloudWatch anomaly detection.

findings.title: Deployment Strategy — No Progressive Delivery
findings.description: GitOps pull-based via Flux CD with rolling updates. No canary or blue/green deployments configured.
findings.gap: No canary or blue/green. Changes applied directly via Flux reconciliation.
findings.recommendation: Add Flagger for canary deployments. Configure automated rollback on failed health checks.

findings.title: Integration Testing — Manual Only
findings.description: Helm test and manual scripts exist but no automated integration tests in CI pipeline.
findings.gap: No automated integration tests. No end-to-end testing of tenant lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Create integration tests for tenant onboarding workflow. Add to CI or scheduled Argo Workflow.

findings.title: Incident Response — No Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation, no self-healing patterns. Argo Workflows available but only used for tenant lifecycle.
findings.gap: Complete absence of incident response automation despite having Argo Workflows infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common scenarios. Implement self-healing via Argo Workflows triggered by Prometheus alerts.

findings.title: Observability Ownership — Not Formalized
findings.description: Kubecost provides per-namespace cost dashboards but no per-service dashboards, no named alarm owners, no CODEOWNERS.
findings.gap: No formalized observability ownership. No team attribution on monitoring resources.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS for observability configs. Create per-tier Grafana dashboards with ownership annotations.

findings.title: Resource Tagging — Inconsistent
findings.description: Basic tagging (Blueprint, Name, karpenter.sh/discovery) but no consistent strategy. No cost allocation, ownership, or environment tags. No enforcement.
findings.gap: Inconsistent tagging. No default_tags in provider. No enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Add default_tags to AWS provider. Add required-tags Config rule. Implement Tag Policies.

evaluations.title: Managed Compute
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — all workloads on EKS with Karpenter; EC2 only for auxiliary Gitea

evaluations.title: Managed Databases
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — all data stores fully managed (DynamoDB with PITR, SQS with SSE)

evaluations.title: Async Messaging and Streaming
evaluations.reason: Score 4 — managed SQS for messaging, Argo Events + NATS EventBus for event-driven patterns

evaluations.reason: Score 4 — DynamoDB is versionless managed service, no EOL concern
